tool_design.role_in_intervention: Workshop structure for defining the team communication charter together.
tool_design.usage_moment: During charter creation or annual refresh
tool_design.expected_output: Completed communication charter with team-agreed norms

failure_warnings.warning: The template is used once as a workshop artifact and then forgotten.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The intervention loses its operating rhythm.
early_signals: The template is completed but never revisited
Actions or notes stay blank after rollout

failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The output exists but does not drive action.
early_signals: No owner or due date captured

content:
# Communication Charter Workshop Template **Tool ID:** T076-02 **Block:** B076 (Communication Culture & Feedback) **Domain:** Team & Talent (D04_team_talent) **Level:** L2 **Format:** Template **Role:** Owner / Manager **Time Required:** 45-60 minutes workshop + 15 minutes to finalise **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For This is not a policy document to write alone and pin on the wall. It is a workshop tool for agreeing how the team will communicate when things are busy, tense, unclear, or uncomfortable. A good charter should answer: - how we raise concerns - how we give feedback - how we disagree - how we handle handovers and missing information - what respectful communication looks like here --- ## Before The Workshop
  • Schedule 45-60 minutes when the team can focus.
  • Bring examples of where communication has recently broken down.
  • Start with the goal: better clarity, not blame.
  • Print one copy of the draft charter for the wall and one working copy for notes.
  • Decide who will facilitate and who will capture wording.
--- ## 45-Minute Workshop Agenda | Time | Section | What To Do | |---|---|---| | 5 min | Why this matters | Explain what better communication protects: guests, safety, team trust, speed, fairness. | | 10 min | What is not working | Ask: where do we get stuck, avoid, snap, or go silent? Capture examples, not names. | | 15 min | Build our rules | Turn team input into short, usable statements. | | 10 min | Pressure test | Ask: would this still help during a rush, conflict, mistake, or rota problem? | | 5 min | Commit and assign | Finalise owner, display location, review date, and how the charter will be used. | --- ## Draft Statement Bank Circle, edit, or replace these. Keep the final wording short enough to remember.
  • We raise concerns early rather than letting them build.
  • We speak directly and respectfully, not through side conversations.
  • We describe behaviour clearly instead of labelling people.
  • We assume good intent first, then ask questions.
  • We do not correct people in front of guests unless safety requires it.
  • We close loops: if something is raised, someone responds.
  • We use handovers, briefings, and notes properly so information does not go missing.
  • We say thank you and recognise good communication, not just mistakes.
--- ## Final Charter Template **Venue:** ______________________________ **Date agreed:** ______________________________ **Review date:** ______________________________ ### How We Communicate Here 1. ________________________________________ 2. ________________________________________ 3. ________________________________________ 4. ________________________________________ 5. ________________________________________ 6. ________________________________________ ### When Something Goes Wrong We will: - ________________________________________ - ________________________________________ - ________________________________________ ### What Respect Looks Like On Shift - ________________________________________ - ________________________________________ - ________________________________________ ### What We Do Not Normalise - ________________________________________ - ________________________________________ - ________________________________________ --- ## Publish It Properly | Task | Owner | Due | |---|---|---| | Final wording agreed | | | | Staff-area poster printed | | | | Charter explained in briefing | | | | New starters shown the charter | | | | Review date booked in diary | | | --- ## Common Mistakes - Writing the charter alone and calling it team-owned. - Making it too long to remember. - Using soft language that nobody can actually apply. - Failing to revisit it when communication slips. - Displaying it publicly but not modelling it privately. --- ## Connected Resources **Used alongside:** - T076-01 - SBI Feedback Quick Card - T076-04 - Difficult Conversations Guide - T076-05 - Communication Culture Survey & Scorecard **Parent block:** B076 - Communication Culture & Feedback --- *Tool Version 2.0 - built to create a living team agreement, not a forgotten poster.*
